task_completion.description: Adjust the upstream boundary condition hydrograph in the HEC-RAS model for the design peak flows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: HEC-RAS Unsteady Flow Data window or hydrograph editor is visible, showing modifications being made to the upstream boundary condition for the Muncie.b04 model.

task_completion.description: Execute the RasUnsteady simulation for the updated scenarios.
task_completion.visual_evidence: HEC-RAS computation window is visible, showing the RasUnsteady simulation actively running or successfully completed (e.g., progress bars or a 'Finished' message).

task_completion.description: Determine the peak water surface elevation (WSE) from the simulation results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: HEC-RAS results windows (such as the Profile Plot, Cross Section plot, or Summary Output Table) are open, displaying the computed peak Water Surface Elevations.

task_completion.description: Export the frequency results to a CSV file with the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'frequency_results.csv' is visible in a text editor or spreadsheet application. It must contain exactly one header row 'return_period,design_flow_cfs,peak_wse_ft' (no spaces around commas) and three data rows ordered by ascending return period (10, 50, 100).

task_completion.description: Record the Base Flood Elevation (100-year peak WSE) to a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'bfe_documentation.txt' is visible in a text editor, containing the text 'BFE=<value>' where the value matches the 100-year peak WSE from the CSV.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually interacted with the HEC-RAS graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the HEC-RAS application open and being used to edit data and run simulations, rather than the agent just using terminal commands or a text editor to fabricate the final output files.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the correct synthetic design flows from the provided text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'design_flow_cfs' column in the final CSV should contain values approximately equal to 16200 (10-yr), 23100 (50-yr), and 26200 (100-yr).

integrity.description: Verify that the output WSE values are physically plausible and logically consistent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'peak_wse_ft' values in the CSV must fall between 935 and 965 feet, and they must be monotonically increasing (the 100-year WSE > 50-year WSE > 10-year WSE).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses synthetic USGS data. The correct design peak flows to be extracted from the report and used in the simulation are approximately 16200 cfs (10-year), 23100 cfs (50-year), and 26200 cfs (100-year). The resulting peak Water Surface Elevation (WSE) values should fall within a plausible range of 935 to 965 feet and must be monotonically increasing (10-yr < 50-yr < 100-yr). The final Base Flood Elevation (BFE) must exactly match the 100-year peak WSE recorded in the CSV.
